VAL-CLI-007.evidence: Ran `./.venv/bin/ornn-bench report tests/fixtures/sample_report.json` and `./.venv/bin/ornn-bench report tests/fixtures/sample_scored_report.json`. Both render a clean Rich-formatted scorecard displaying: Ornn-I (Inference): 92.5, Ornn-T (Training): 88.3, component metrics (Memory Bandwidth BW: 1.2500, FP8 Compute: 0.9500, BF16 Compute: 1.1000, All-Reduce AR: 0.8500), and qualification outcome (★ Premium) with Score Status (✓ Valid). Output uses well-structured Rich panels and tables with clear section headers (Report Metadata, Sections, Ornn Scores, Component Metrics, Qualification). Exit code 0.
VAL-CLI-007.details: Scorecard displays all required elements: Ornn-I, Ornn-T, all four component metrics (bw, fp8, bf16, ar), and qualification badge (Premium) with clean readable formatting. Both sample_report.json and sample_scored_report.json render identically. Plain text mode (--plain) also displays all required fields in a clean ASCII format without box-drawing characters. 20 automated tests in test_report_command.py all pass.

VAL-CLI-008.evidence: Ran `ornn-bench report tests/fixtures/sample_report.json` on macOS host without NVIDIA GPU tools (no nvidia-smi, no CUDA). Command succeeded with exit code 0 and rendered the full scorecard including all sections, scores, component metrics, and qualification. Also tested with --json and --plain flags, all exit code 0. No GPU dependency errors or tracebacks.
VAL-CLI-008.details: The report command successfully re-renders saved reports on non-GPU machines. Tested both fixture files (sample_report.json and sample_scored_report.json). All three output modes (rich/default, --json, --plain) work without GPU tools. Exit code 0 in all cases. 20 automated tests in test_report_command.py confirm this behavior.

steps.expected: Plain text output without GPU errors
steps.observed: Clean plain text output, exit code 0

steps.expected: Scored report renders without GPU errors

VAL-CLI-011.evidence: Tested three non-TTY/machine-readable scenarios: (1) --json flag outputs valid parseable JSON with zero ANSI escape sequences (verified via python json.tool and byte-level ESC scan showing 0 \x1b bytes). (2) --plain flag outputs clean ASCII text with zero ANSI escape sequences. (3) Piped output (| cat) contains zero ANSI escape sequences (confirmed via od -c and python byte scan). Box-drawing characters in piped default output are UTF-8 Unicode, not ANSI escapes. JSON output contains all expected fields (scores.ornn_i, scores.ornn_t, scores.qualification). 19 automated tests in test_non_tty_output.py all pass.
VAL-CLI-011.details: JSON mode produces machine-readable output that parses cleanly with json.loads(). Plain mode produces clean ASCII without control characters. Piped output has no ANSI CSI sequences (\x1b[). The default rich output when piped contains Unicode box-drawing characters but zero ANSI color/formatting escapes, making it safe for log capture. All 19 non-TTY output tests pass.

steps.observed: JSON parsed successfully, contains all expected keys

VAL-CLI-011: JSON output is valid parseable JSON, plain text is clean ASCII, and piped output contains zero ANSI escape sequences. All 39 automated tests (20 report + 19 non-TTY) pass.
